name: is_end_always_true
reason: If entailed, every KeyIterator would be considered at-end, making get() unreachable and the iterator useless.

name: some_reported_as_end
code:
proof fn phi_2_some_reported_as_end<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(iter: &KeyIterator<K>, v: K)
    requires
        iter.k == Some(v),
    ensures
        iter.is_end_spec(),
{
}
reason: If entailed, a KeyIterator holding a valid key would be classified as at-end, contradicting the Some/None semantics.

name: get_mismatches_key
code:
proof fn phi_3_get_mismatches_key<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(iter: &KeyIterator<K>, v: K)
    requires
        iter.k == Some(v),
    ensures
        *iter.get_spec() != v,
{
}
reason: If entailed, get() would return a value different from the stored key, breaking the fundamental accessor contract.

name: non_end_implies_end
code:
proof fn phi_4_non_end_implies_end<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(iter: &KeyIterator<K>)
    requires
        !iter.is_end_spec(),
    ensures
        iter.is_end_spec(),
{
}
reason: If entailed, the is_end spec would be self-contradictory, meaning the KeyIterator type is uninhabitable in non-end states.

name: get_conflates_distinct_keys
code:
proof fn phi_5_get_conflates_distinct_keys<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(
    iter1: &KeyIterator<K>,
    iter2: &KeyIterator<K>,
    v1: K,
    v2: K,
)
    requires
        iter1.k == Some(v1),
        iter2.k == Some(v2),
        v1 != v2,
    ensures
        *iter1.get_spec() == *iter2.get_spec(),
{
}
reason: If entailed, get() could not distinguish different keys, meaning the spec collapses all key values to a single equivalence class.
